user.title: OKTE EDC mailbox
user.description:
Enter the IMAP credentials for the mailbox receiving OKTE settlement emails.

⚠️ **Early-version, unofficial integration — use at your own risk.** It logs in with write access (it can flag, move, or delete messages depending on the cleanup mode you choose), stores your IMAP credentials in Home Assistant, and writes long-term statistics into the HA recorder. The values it produces are not suitable for billing or regulatory purposes without independent reconciliation. Recommended before continuing: use a dedicated mailbox you can afford to lose, back up your HA configuration and recorder database, and review the [README and source code](https://github.com/vaind/ha-okte-edc).

folder.title: Choose IMAP folder
folder.description: Choose the folder OKTE settlement emails are delivered to.

discover_eics.title: Choose metering points
discover_eics.description:
Each EIC is a single metering point — one electricity meter at a specific address. Pick the EICs that belong to the location this Home Assistant install represents:

- **Producer + its off-take at the same address** (typical PV-sharing setup): import both. Together they paint the complete grid / PV / sharing picture for one home.
- **EICs at a different address** (for example you also forward a relative's mailbox into this account): leave them unchecked. You can install a second integration instance later for that location.

Heads up: once an EIC is imported and statistics start being written, disabling it later stops new imports but leaves the historical data in the HA recorder. It's easier to skip an EIC now than to clean it up later.

post_setup.title: Almost done
post_setup.description:
Setup is ready to go. The EICs you selected will be imported on the next polling cycle.

**Wire the sensors into the Energy dashboard** (Settings → Dashboards → Energy — see the [official Home Assistant Energy docs](https://www.home-assistant.io/docs/energy/)):

- Off-take `grid_import` → **Grid consumption**
- Off-take `shared_in` → **Solar production** (the sharing scheme acts as a free-energy source from the off-taker's side)
- Producer `grid_return` → **Return to grid**

If you have multiple EICs at the same physical address, add each one's sensor to the same slot — Home Assistant sums them. EICs at different addresses should generally not be combined; either skip them or install a separate integration instance for the other location.

The `total_*` sensors are informational reference only and don't map to a dashboard slot.

This guide also lives permanently in the [README's Energy dashboard mapping section](https://github.com/vaind/ha-okte-edc#energy-dashboard-mapping) — accessible from the integration's *Documentation* button at any time.

reauth_confirm.title: Re-authenticate OKTE EDC
reauth_confirm.description: The IMAP credentials no longer work. Enter the new password.

error.cannot_connect: Could not connect to the IMAP server.
error.folder_not_found: The selected folder does not exist on the server.
error.no_eics_found: No OKTE emails found in this mailbox in the configured scan window.

abort.cannot_connect: Could not connect to the IMAP server.
abort.no_eics_found: No OKTE emails found in this mailbox.
